description_long: This object is a military-style airplane with a streamlined fuselage and a camouflage paint scheme in shades of green and brown. The wings are straight and mounted at a mid-level position on the fuselage, with two jet engines housed in nacelles beneath each wing. The tail section features a conventional design with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers extending from either side. The cockpit is located at the front of the fuselage and appears to be enclosed with a clear canopy. The surface of the airplane is hard and matte, with visible panel lines and a slightly angular geometry typical of mid-20th-century jet aircraft.
description: A camouflaged, mid-wing military jet airplane with twin underwing engines, a conventional tail, and an enclosed forward cockpit.
description_view_0: The airplane is seen from a rear-side angle, highlighting the twin jet engines under the wings and the single vertical stabilizer at the tail.
description_view_1: A direct rear view shows the symmetry of the wings, the placement of the engines, and the full tail assembly.
description_view_2: The airplane is viewed from the opposite rear-side, again showing the engines, tail, and the angular camouflage pattern.
description_view_3: A direct front view reveals the nose, cockpit canopy, and the forward-facing jet intakes beneath the wings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing configuration with straight wings, no visible winglets, and no pronounced dihedral or anhedral angle.
attribute_engine_type: Twin jet engines mounted under the wings.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Not visibly extended; likely retractable landing gear based on typical design, but not confirmed in images.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a single clear canopy.
